title: Active-active multi-region serverless PostgreSQL with Amazon Aurora DSQL and AWS Lambda
description: Deploy Amazon Aurora DSQL linked clusters across two regions with an AWS Lambda function that demonstrates active-active reads and writes using IAM authentication. No passwords, no connection pooling, no VPC required.

introBox.headline: How it works
text: Amazon Aurora DSQL is a serverless, distributed SQL database with active-active multi-region support and PostgreSQL compatibility.
This pattern deploys linked Amazon Aurora DSQL clusters in two AWS regions that replicate writes automatically with strong consistency.
The AWS Lambda function connects using IAM authentication tokens (no passwords to manage) and demonstrates writing in one region and reading the replicated data in the peer region.
Unlike traditional Amazon RDS or Amazon Aurora, Amazon Aurora DSQL requires no VPC, no connection pooling (Amazon RDS Proxy), and no credential rotation -- IAM auth handles everything.

bullets.text: Connecting to Amazon Aurora DSQL with IAM authentication

text: See the GitHub repo for detailed testing instructions.
